div.head: Minister Dawson to the Secretary of State.
div.p: Mr. Dawson states that the documents referred to in his telegram of October 28, signed, and one original of each was delivered to him, and that the note from the foreign office will also set forth the program, including a mixed commission for all unliquidated claims, one member to be named by Nicaragua, a second by the United States, and, if a third be necessary, to be chosen by the first two, the department to have the right to modify this feature; also requesting loan and the sending of a financial expert who should leave the united States about December 15. Adds that the leaders have further signed and handed to him, as witness, a personal arrangement agreeing to support in 1912 any candidate the majority of themselves may agree upon.
